channel.title: Posts on Not a Peeker of Nature
channel.description: Recent content in Posts on Not a Peeker of Nature

item.title: Probability and statistics
item.description: #1 The basis of probability reference link #2 Random variable reference link #3 Discrete random variable reference link #4 Continuous random variable reference link #5 Normal distribution reference link #6 Joint probability distribution (1) reference link #7 Joint probability distribution (2) reference link #8 Joint probability distribution (3) reference link #9 Joint probability distribution (4) reference link #10 Typical distributions (t distribution, chi-square distribution, F distribution) reference link #11 From probability to statistics reference link

item.title: Signal processing
Techniques
item.description: #ChIP-seq Digital signal processing #RNA-seq To be continued …

item.title: Quickstart of wsl2
item.description: #Install wsl2 I usually use VPN, so not only will it require me to set the network (wifi/ethernet), but also require me to set the DNS of the wsl2 as 8.8.8.8 and 8.8.4.4 #Install conda Why? Configuring environment is quite important, and sometimes annoying especially for a beginner. The issues can be various, so I’d like to collect them in the post. There are two main benefits for me: one is to save time if I have the same issue next time, another is to build a framework of virtual environment so that I can really understand how it works.

item.title: Tools in bioinformatics
item.description: #Dataset Multiomics (bulk cell): bulk ATAC-seq database https://academic.oup.com/nar/article/49/D1/D55/5943839 bulk ChIP-seq and ATAC-seq database https://chip-atlas.org/ Other ATAC-seq or ChIP-seq data can be downloaded from GEO (https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/geo/) or Encode https://www.encodeproject.org/matrix/?type=Experiment&control_type!=*&status=released&perturbed=false roadmap (https://egg2.wustl.edu/roadmap) Multiomics (single cell): single cell database: https://data.humancellatlas.org/ human tumor: https://humantumoratlas.org/ Genome and annotations: UCSC for genome https://hgdownload.soe.ucsc.edu/downloads.html (eg. https://hgdownload.soe.ucsc.edu/goldenPath/dm3/) Ensembl for refgenes annotations https://ftp.ensembl.org/pub/ sybapse ypf2944164500@gmail.com @ypf19950216 Summary of online resources in genomics Appendix1: Kingfisher is a package for downloading fastq data

item.title: Tools in bioinformatics
item.description: #Aspera Why use aspera: a. Reliability and integrity (ensures complete data integrity with checks at every packet transfer point and automatic retry mechanisms that handle intermittent network issues without any data loss or corruption.); b. High-speed transfers Installation on linux: We can use conda or source code to install. Here we choose conda to install (reference link) An example of using aspera to download fastq data When I studied the docker-based Hi-C pipeline CustardPy, I needed to download a series of fastq data: SRR17870713, SRR17870718, SRR17870740.

item.title: Basis_of_computer
item.description: #BIOS BIOS stands for ‘Basic Input/Output System’. Whether SVM mode (for AMD; VT-x for Intel) is enabled in BIOS can affect the functionality of wsl2. #Computer hardware Key components to assemble a computer and their functions: 1. Motherboard The motherboard is the main printed circuit board (PCB) in a computer. It is the central communication backbone connectivity point through which all components and external peripherals connect. Functions: Component Integration: The motherboard houses the CPU, memory (RAM), and provides connectors for other peripherals like the graphics card, hard drives, and optical drives.

item.title: Skills to learn (1)
item.description: #Basis of github #Routine pipelines 1. RNA-seq: https://rumball.readthedocs.io/en/latest/ 2. ChIP-seq: https://churros.readthedocs.io/en/latest/ 3. Hi-C: https://custardpy.readthedocs.io/en/latest/ 4. Single-cell: https://github.com/rnakato/ShortCake #Routine analysis DEG analysis data_downloaded #Docker and singularity Introduction Docker VS singularity: are both containerization platforms, competitors. Docker/singularity: manage containers which contain packages, depencies, scripts, even data; Conda: manage virtual environments which contain packages. Installation For window, desk-top version of docker is recommended; If we use VPN in mainland China, we had better change DNS of wsl2 and network (wifi/ethernet) into that of Google (8.

item.title: Routine_analysis_in_bioinformatics
item.description: Why? There are two main reasons why I want to record these routine pipelines: (1) save some time if Routine pipeline 1: RNA-seq (ref: https://rumball.readthedocs.io/en/latest/) To be continued …

item.title: Routine_pipelines_in_bioinformatics
item.description: Why? There are lots of packages dealing with various routine analysis in bioinformatics. Many of them are R packages Routine analysis 1: volcano plot To be continued …

item.title: Nomenclature of genes and proteins
item.description: Why? When I wrote my thesis, I did not write the gene names in italics. Here is the summary I find in Zhihu. I think the summary is clear and enough for me.

item.title: SSH service
item.description: SSH service Why? There are two main reasons for me to start SSH service on WSL: It’s convenient for me to operate my host machine in the lab. As long as there is a low-end, lightweight MacBook, we can deal with some tasks with SSH connecting to my host machine How? I tried many tutorials on the Internet, most of them don’t work. So, I want to record how to do it step by step (Reference blog).

item.title: HUGO, follow-up
item.description: Hello, HUGO! Why? Inspired by the website of Prof.XiChen, I also want to build my own website using HUGO with gokarna theme. I think it’s a good way to record my thoughts and progresses, as well as a method to present myself. Besides, it can also encourage me to write more, which I think, is quite important to reorganize various pieces of knowledge and assimilate them into my own understanding.

item.title: Hello, HUGO!
item.description: Hello, HUGO! Why? Inspired by the website of Prof. Xi Chen, I also want to build my own website using HUGO with gokarna. I think it’s a good way to record my thoughts and progresses, as well as a method to present myself. Besides, it can also encourage me to write more, which I think, is quite important to reorganize various pieces of knowledge and assimilate them into my own understanding.

item.title: Draw figures for publications
item.description: Why? Usually, I use Python with ggplot style to draw plots and take screenshots of date displayed in browser. Then, I organize them into a full figure in PPT. But there are two problems that trouble me: one is the low resolution, and the other is the inconvenience when converting from one layout to another. When I read the post “Drawing Vector Graphics For Publication Figures” published by Prof. Xi Chen, I realized there are some better methods.
